---
title: "HWE chi-square eider duck"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
# Hardy-Weinberg Equilibrium Chi-Squared Test
# Eider duck protein polymorphism data

# Observed genotype counts
obs_FF <- 37
obs_FS <- 24
obs_SS <- 6
N <- obs_FF + obs_FS + obs_SS  # Total individuals = 67

# Step 1: Calculate allele frequencies
total_alleles <- 2 * N
freq_F <- (2 * obs_FF + obs_FS) / total_alleles  # p
freq_S <- (obs_FS + 2 * obs_SS) / total_alleles  # q

cat("Allele Frequencies:\n")
cat(sprintf("p (F allele) = %.4f\n", freq_F))
cat(sprintf("q (S allele) = %.4f\n", freq_S))
cat(sprintf("p + q = %.4f\n\n", freq_F + freq_S))

# Step 2: Calculate expected genotype frequencies under HWE
exp_FF <- N * freq_F^2
exp_FS <- N * 2 * freq_F * freq_S
exp_SS <- N * freq_S^2

cat("Expected Genotype Counts (under HWE):\n")
cat(sprintf("FF: %.2f\n", exp_FF))
cat(sprintf("FS: %.2f\n", exp_FS))
cat(sprintf("SS: %.2f\n\n", exp_SS))

# Step 3: Chi-squared test using chisq.test()
observed <- c(obs_FF, obs_FS, obs_SS)
expected <- c(exp_FF, exp_FS, exp_SS)

# Create a summary table
results_table <- data.frame(
  Genotype = c("FF", "FS", "SS"),
  Observed = observed,
  Expected = round(expected, 2),
  Difference = observed - expected,
  Chi_sq_component = (observed - expected)^2 / expected
)

cat("Chi-Squared Analysis Table:\n")
print(results_table)
cat("\n")

# Perform chi-squared test
# Note: We use df = 1 because we estimated 1 parameter (p) from the data
chi_test <- chisq.test(observed, p = expected/N, rescale.p = TRUE)

cat("Chi-Squared Test Results:\n")
cat(sprintf("χ² statistic: %.4f\n", chi_test$statistic))
cat(sprintf("Degrees of freedom: %d\n", chi_test$parameter))
cat(sprintf("P-value: %.4f\n\n", chi_test$p.value))

# Alternative: Manual calculation
chi_sq_manual <- sum((observed - expected)^2 / expected)
p_value_manual <- pchisq(chi_sq_manual, df = 1, lower.tail = FALSE)

cat("Manual Calculation (for verification):\n")
cat(sprintf("χ² statistic: %.4f\n", chi_sq_manual))
cat(sprintf("P-value (df=1): %.4f\n\n", p_value_manual))

# Conclusion
alpha <- 0.05
critical_value <- qchisq(1 - alpha, df = 1)

cat("Statistical Decision:\n")
cat(sprintf("Significance level (α): %.2f\n", alpha))
cat(sprintf("Critical value (χ²₀.₀₅,₁): %.3f\n", critical_value))

if (chi_sq_manual < critical_value) {
  cat("\nConclusion: Fail to reject the null hypothesis.\n")
  cat("The population appears to be in Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium.\n")
} else {
  cat("\nConclusion: Reject the null hypothesis.\n")
  cat("The population shows significant deviation from Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium.\n")
}
```
